module-1-field-notes-repository.label: Module Project: Start Volunteer Project History
module-1-field-notes-repository.summary: Begin the two-file volunteer-project repository and record its first snapshot.

try_git_repo_inspection.title: Inspect the Volunteer Project Folder
try_git_repo_inspection.prompt: The parallel project opens inside `volunteer-project/`. Confirm the current directory and list its two plain-text files: `README.md` and `assignments.txt`. Do not initialize Git yet.
try_git_repo_inspection.hint: Verify the project root and its small two-file starting set.

help.concept: `pwd` prints the current directory. `ls` lists visible files in that directory. These terminal commands inspect the ordinary folder before Git is initialized.
help.hint_2: The listing should contain only the two learner files.

try_git_repo_inspection.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_repo_inspection.instructions: The parallel project opens inside `volunteer-project/`. Confirm the current directory and list its two plain-text files: `README.md` and `assignments.txt`. Do not initialize Git yet.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned

Did you confirm the current project directory?

1.message: Did you list the two supplied text files?

try_git_config.title: Initialize the Volunteer Project
try_git_config.prompt: The two-file folder is ready and still has no repository metadata. Initialize it on `main` without changing either file.
try_git_config.hint: Reapply the initialization command from the guided project.

help.concept: `git init -b main` creates repository metadata in the current folder and selects `main` as the first branch. The files remain untracked until you stage them.

try_git_config.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_config.instructions: The two-file folder is ready and still has no repository metadata. Initialize it on `main` without changing either file.

try_git_init.title: Configure the Volunteer Project Identity
try_git_init.prompt: Store the supplied name `ZoeSkoul Learner` and email `learner@zoeskoul.local` in this repository only.
try_git_init.hint: Use the same two repository-local settings as the guided project.

help.concept: `git config --local user.name VALUE` and `git config --local user.email VALUE` prepare this independent repository to record commits without changing global Git settings.

try_git_init.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_init.solutionCode:
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
try_git_init.instructions: Store the supplied name `ZoeSkoul Learner` and email `learner@zoeskoul.local` in this repository only.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt

try_git_stage_files.title: Prepare the Volunteer Baseline
try_git_stage_files.prompt: Stage only `README.md` and `assignments.txt`, then inspect the staged statistics before committing.
try_git_stage_files.hint: Prepare the complete two-file baseline and review it.

help.concept: `git add README.md assignments.txt` stages both project files. `git diff --staged --stat` confirms which files the first snapshot would contain without recording it.
help.hint_1: Do not stage hidden platform files.
help.hint_2: The staged summary should contain exactly two files.

try_git_stage_files.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_stage_files.solutionCode:
git add README.md assignments.txt
git diff --staged --stat
try_git_stage_files.instructions: Stage only `README.md` and `assignments.txt`, then inspect the staged statistics before committing.

git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local

try_git_commit.title: Record and Verify the Volunteer Baseline
try_git_commit.prompt: Record the prepared two-file snapshot with the message `Start volunteer project`. Then inspect concise history and confirm that short status has no unfinished entries.
try_git_commit.hint: Commit once, then verify history and current state.

help.concept: `git commit -m ...` records the staged files. `git log --oneline` verifies the history entry, and `git status --short` verifies that no tracked work remains unfinished.
help.hint_2: The final short status should be empty.

try_git_commit.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_commit.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Start volunteer project"
git log --oneline
git status --short
try_git_commit.instructions: Record the prepared two-file snapshot with the message `Start volunteer project`. Then inspect concise history and confirm that short status has no unfinished entries.

moduleProject.title: Build the Volunteer Project Baseline
moduleProject.description: This project starts with only README.md and assignments.txt. Reapply the Module 1 workflow in a different repository, then carry that exact history into later module projects.

intro-to-git-repository.title: Parallel Project: Volunteer Project
intro-to-git-repository.bodyMarkdown: `volunteer-project/` begins with only `README.md` and `assignments.txt`. Reapply the Module 1 workflow in a different two-file project: inspect the folder, initialize Git, configure the local identity, stage both files, commit, and verify. The repository continues into later module projects.
